title: Detecting Cycles in a Graph: Edge Addition

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Given an empty graph with n nodes, add m undirected edges to it. Then, return true if the graph contains a cycle, false otherwise.
statement.outputFormat: Return true if the graph contains a cycle, false otherwise.

hints.text: Implement a cycle detection method within the graph class to identify cycles after adding edges.

editorial.title: Graph Cycle Detection
editorial.intuition: To detect cycles in a graph, we can use a depth-first search (DFS) approach.
editorial.bruteForce: We can attempt to detect cycles by trying all possible pairs of edges and checking if their removal results in a cycle.
editorial.betterSolution: We can optimize this approach by implementing a cycle detection method within the graph class.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution uses a boolean array to keep track of visited nodes during DFS.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness is proven by demonstrating that our approach correctly identifies cycles in all possible cases.
editorial.dryRun: We can test our code by running it on various input scenarios and verifying that it produces the expected output.
commonMistakes: Not using an efficient cycle detection method.

starterCode.cpp: // Define a Graph class with methods to add edges and detect cycles.
starterCode.java: // Define a Graph class with methods to add edges and detect cycles.
starterCode.javascript: // Define a Graph class with methods to add edges and detect cycles.
